config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan kebebasan dan martabat orang per orang. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai lewat keseimbangan yang adil, bukan dengan mengorbankan hak dasar individu begitu saja. Kalau hak individu terlalu mudah diabaikan, justru masyarakat bisa jadi tidak sehat dan tidak aman dalam jangka panjang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society’s interests should automatically come first over individual rights in the United States. Our system is built on the idea that individual rights are a check on majoritarian power, because “the public good” can easily become an excuse for abuse, discrimination, or government overreach. That said, rights are not absolute in every case; public safety, public health, and due process sometimes justify limits, but those limits have to be narrow and justified. If we reverse the priority too casually, we end up treating people as means to an end instead of citizens with inherent dignity.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang malah menimpa kelompok kecil, warga biasa, atau suara yang tidak populer. Kepentingan bersama itu penting, tetapi justru akan lebih sehat kalau hak dasar individu dipakai sebagai pagar, bukan dikorbankan dulu baru dicari pembenarannya. Bagi saya, masyarakat kuat bukan yang paling sering mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi keduanya secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule, because in the United States that kind of logic has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. The strongest part of the other side's argument is that public safety and public health sometimes require limits, but those limits should be exceptional, narrow, and subject to strict scrutiny. If we start with society-first as the default, then individual rights stop functioning as real protections and become privileges the majority can suspend whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari konteks Indonesia, saya justru melihat label “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai terlalu longgar untuk menekan warga kecil, minoritas, atau orang yang suaranya tidak kuat. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti bencana, kesehatan publik, atau keamanan yang bisa membatasi hak, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan dijadikan alasan default. Kalau hak individu terlalu mudah dikalahkan, masyarakat tidak jadi lebih baik; yang ada malah membuka jalan untuk penyalahgunaan kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From an American perspective, that framing is dangerous because it has historically been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially when the majority claims to know what is best for everyone else. Public safety and public health can justify limits in specific cases, but those limits need to be narrow, transparent, and accountable rather than treated as the default. If we make society-first the rule, individual rights stop being real protections and become permissions the powerful can revoke whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
